## Highlights

Five new locked decisions land in this release — model-tier cost knob, adopt-existing-solution rule, strict subagent-return schema, skill-runner boundary, and a `/ginee-update` bug fix that unblocked the standard install path.

### Added

- **D31 — Per-role + per-task model tier** ([#76](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/issues/76) · [PR #78](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/pull/78)). Routes reasoning-heavy roles to capable models and execution-heavy roles to cheaper ones. Three vendor-neutral tiers (`reasoning` · `standard` · `fast`) declared as role-kernel `default-tier:`; concrete model IDs live only in the adapter layer. Defaults: `team-lead` + `solution-architect` → `reasoning`; `ai-engineer` + 4 engineer cardinals → `standard`. Per-task prefix `model:<tier>` combinable with `auto:` / `branch:` / `wt:` / `commit:`. Claude install writes `model: <id>` into each `.claude/agents/<role>.md` frontmatter; non-Claude adapters emit a one-line install warning + document the prefix as a user hint. Purely additive — absent `model-tier:` config → framework defaults apply silently.
- **D30 — Adopt-existing-solution as a first-class Phase-2 option** ([#75](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/issues/75) · [PR #77](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/pull/77)). Every Phase 2 design proposal AND every iteration-protocol Propose step (Phase 4–7 > 15-min sub-tasks with a live adopt-vs-build axis) MUST surface ≥ 1 adopt candidate — with `name · version · source link · license · one-line fit rationale` — OR an explicit `(none viable — <reason>)` cite. 4 candidate types (`adopt` / `build` / `hybrid` / `(none viable)`); 5 mandatory checks before surfacing; LLM self-review enforcement (no external linter). Same machinery as D22 / D26 / D29. Closes the LLM-default failure mode of authoring novel implementations when no rule forces the proposer to look outward first.
- **D29 — Strict subagent-return schema** ([#69](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/issues/69) · [PR #74](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/pull/74)). Binds every cardinal-dispatch return to a strict schema per `core/templates/phase-report.md`. 5 mandatory sections (`## Files touched` · `## Decisions made` · `## Verification log` · `## Open issues` · `## Next dispatch needed`) + 2 conditional + 1 optional escape hatch. 6 mandatory checks (5 from D22 / D26 + *no narrative preamble*). Measured 68.5% byte reduction on a real Phase-4 return (3,603 → 1,136 chars). Cardinal returns were the largest single contributor to orchestration-thread bloat pre-D29.
- **D28 — Skill-runner / team-lead surface boundary** ([#71](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/issues/71) · [PR #72](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/pull/72)). Locks the structural rule that prevents the skill-runner main thread from orchestrating. Skill-runner = thin mechanical surface running a `ginee-*` skill body. Allowed: parse / identify source · label · sticky · audit-comment ops · branch ops · the skill's one named first-batch dispatch. Forbidden (must dispatch `@team-lead`): plan drafting · synthesis of parallel returns · gate text · re-dispatch · routing reconciliation · default selection · `local/bindings.md` lookup to settle routing. Hand-back rule: every `ginee-*` skill dispatches `@team-lead` after its first mechanical batch.

### Fixed

- **D27 — `/ginee-update` now reaches a standard install** ([#67](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/issues/67) · [PR #70](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/pull/70)). Pre-D27, `/ginee-update` required `install.{ps1,sh}` inside `.agents/ginee/` — but the bootstrap intentionally prunes them (they belong to the deploy layer, not runtime). Every standard install therefore exited at Step 1. Step 1 now requires **only** `<fw>/core/VERSION`; Step 6 fetches `install.{ps1,sh}` from `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<framework-repo>/<ref>/` to a temp dir and runs them with `-Target` / `-Adapter` / `-Ref` / `-RepoUrl` / `-UpdateOnly`. Installer itself unchanged.

## Adopter migration

**None required** for D27, D28, D29, D30, D31 — all purely additive. No `local/*` schema changes; no new commands; no adapter re-install needed.

**One-time recovery for D27** — pre-D27 installs whose `/ginee-update` was broken can land the fix via the bootstrap one-liner once:

```powershell
$env:GINEE_UPDATE_ONLY='1'; $env:GINEE_ADAPTER='claude'; iwr -useb https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/main/install.ps1 | iex
```

```bash
GINEE_UPDATE_ONLY=1 GINEE_ADAPTER=claude bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/main/install.sh)"
```

Subsequent updates flow through the fixed `/ginee-update`.
